seoTitle: Galaxycore Authorized Distributor | CMOS Image Sensors & Display Drivers | LiTong Electronics
seoDescription: Authorized distributor of Galaxycore CMOS image sensors and display driver ICs. Comprehensive product selection, technical support, and application solutions for mobile, automotive, and security imaging.

faqs.question: What are the main product categories offered by Galaxycore?
faqs.answer: Galaxycore offers 4 core product categories: CMOS Image Sensors, Display Driver ICs, Automotive Image Sensors, and Security & IoT Sensors. Each category offers multiple series optimized for specific applications and performance requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Click on any product category to explore detailed specifications, available models, and application guidance.

faqs.question: Is LiTong Electronics an authorized Galaxycore distributor?
faqs.answer: Yes, LiTong Electronics is an authorized distributor of Galaxycore products. We maintain direct partnerships with Galaxycore to ensure our customers receive genuine products with full manufacturer warranty and technical support. As an authorized distributor, we have access to Galaxycore's complete product portfolio, including their latest CMOS image sensors and display driver ICs. Our partnership enables us to provide competitive pricing, reliable supply chain management, and comprehensive technical support for your imaging and display applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: When purchasing Galaxycore products, always verify that your supplier is an authorized distributor to ensure product authenticity and warranty coverage. Contact us for a copy of our authorization certificate.

faqs.question: What are Galaxycore's core competitive advantages in the image sensor market?
faqs.answer: Galaxycore has established several key competitive advantages in the CMOS image sensor market. First, their strong R&D capabilities enable rapid innovation in pixel technology and image processing algorithms. Second, their cost-effective manufacturing partnerships allow competitive pricing without compromising quality. Third, their extensive product portfolio covers the full spectrum from entry-level to high-end imaging solutions. Fourth, their deep understanding of the Chinese and Asian markets enables better localization support. Finally, their automotive-grade product line meets stringent AEC-Q100 standards, making them a trusted supplier for automotive OEMs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider Galaxycore for projects requiring cost-effective imaging solutions with good performance, especially for mobile, automotive, and security applications. Their local support and rapid response times are significant advantages for Asia-based projects.

faqs.question: How do Galaxycore products compare to other image sensor brands?
faqs.answer: Galaxycore products offer an excellent balance of performance and cost-effectiveness compared to other major brands. In the mobile segment, their sensors compete favorably with Sony and Samsung in the mid-range market while offering better value. For automotive applications, their AEC-Q100 qualified sensors provide comparable performance to ON Semiconductor and Omnivision at more competitive price points. In the security camera market, their low-light performance and HDR capabilities match industry standards. While Sony maintains leadership in the highest-end flagship sensors, Galaxycore dominates the high-volume mid-range segment with reliable, cost-effective solutions.
faqs.decisionGuide: For cost-sensitive applications requiring good image quality, Galaxycore offers compelling alternatives to premium brands. Contact our FAE team for detailed comparison data and sample evaluation.

faqs.question: How do I select the right Galaxycore image sensor for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right Galaxycore image sensor involves evaluating several key parameters. First, determine your resolution requirements based on the target application - 2MP-5MP for basic security, 8MP-13MP for smartphones, and higher resolutions for professional applications. Second, consider the optical format (1/4", 1/3", 1/2.8") to match your lens selection. Third, evaluate low-light performance requirements and select sensors with appropriate pixel size and sensitivity. Fourth, check for required features like HDR, autofocus support, and high frame rates. Fifth, for automotive applications, ensure AEC-Q100 qualification. Finally, consider interface requirements (MIPI CSI-2, DVP) and power consumption constraints.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use our product selection guide or contact our FAE team with your specific requirements. We can provide comparison matrices, sample evaluation, and recommendations based on your application needs.

categories.name: CMOS Image Sensors
categories.description: High-performance CMOS image sensors ranging from 2MP to 200MP for mobile, automotive, and security applications. Galaxycore's CMOS sensors offer excellent image quality with competitive pricing, suitable for smartphone cameras, automotive systems, and security solutions. As a Galaxycore distributor, LiTong Electronics provides selection guidance, samples, and technical support for these high-resolution sensors.
categories.longDescription: Galaxycore's CMOS Image Sensors represent cutting-edge technology in imaging solutions. These sensors are designed for a wide range of applications from mobile devices to automotive systems. As an authorized Galaxycore distributor, we provide access to a complete product range including the GC08A3 8MP sensor for mobile and GC13A0 13MP sensor for smartphones. Our product selection includes features like high sensitivity, low noise, and advanced image processing capabilities. With our expert technical support, customers can easily find the right CMOS sensor for their specific application requirements.
categories.selectionGuide: Comprehensive guide to choosing the optimal Galaxycore CMOS image sensor based on your application requirements

faqs.question: What are the key specifications of Galaxycore's CMOS sensors?
faqs.answer: Galaxycore's CMOS sensors feature resolutions from 2MP up to 200MP, with optical formats ranging from 1/4" to 1/1.2". Pixel sizes typically range from 1.12μm to 2.0μm. The sensors support various interfaces including MIPI CSI-2, DVP, and parallel output. Features include HDR capabilities, low-light performance, and advanced image processing algorithms that enhance color accuracy and dynamic range.
faqs.decisionGuide: Match sensor specifications to your application needs - higher resolution for professional photography, smaller pixel sizes for improved low-light performance, and appropriate interfaces for your system design.

faqs.question: Which CMOS sensors are best for smartphone applications?
faqs.answer: For smartphone applications, Galaxycore's 8MP GC08A3 and 13MP GC13A0 sensors are ideal choices. These sensors offer excellent image quality with optimized power consumption and compact packaging. The GC08A3 provides good performance for mid-range smartphones, while the GC13A0 is designed for premium smartphone cameras. Both sensors support standard smartphone interfaces and feature low-light performance suitable for consumer photography.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose the GC08A3 for cost-effective solutions or the GC13A0 for premium smartphone applications. Consider your target market and required features when making the selection.

faqs.question: What packaging options are available for CMOS sensors?
faqs.answer: Galaxycore's CMOS sensors are available in various packaging options including QFN, CSP, and LGA formats. These packages are designed to meet different requirements for space constraints, heat dissipation, and mounting flexibility. For mobile applications, ultra-small packages like CSP are preferred to minimize module thickness. Automotive-grade sensors may use more robust packages for enhanced durability in harsh environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select packaging based on your application's space constraints, thermal requirements, and mounting method. Consider thermal performance and reliability for automotive or industrial applications.

faqs.question: Can Galaxycore CMOS sensors be used in automotive applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, Galaxycore offers automotive-grade CMOS sensors that meet AEC-Q100 qualification standards for automotive applications. These sensors are designed with enhanced reliability and performance characteristics for use in vehicle systems. While our current focus is on 2MP and 4MP automotive sensors, future developments may include higher resolution options for advanced automotive applications such as ADAS and surround view systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: For automotive applications, ensure that the sensor meets AEC-Q100 standards for automotive use. Contact our automotive FAE team for detailed specifications and qualification details.

faqs.question: How does Galaxycore compare to other CMOS sensor providers?
faqs.answer: Galaxycore stands out in the CMOS sensor market by offering competitive pricing while maintaining high image quality. Compared to Sony and Samsung, Galaxycore's sensors provide excellent value in the mid-range segment, particularly for smartphone applications. Their automotive-grade products meet industry standards like AEC-Q100, offering reliability similar to ON Semiconductor and Omnivision. In security cameras, Galaxycore sensors deliver competitive low-light performance with HDR capabilities that match leading brands.
faqs.decisionGuide: For cost-sensitive projects with good performance requirements, Galaxycore offers an excellent balance. For high-end flagship sensors, Sony may still be preferred, but Galaxycore excels in mid-range applications.

faqs.question: What are the typical applications for CMOS sensors?
faqs.answer: Galaxycore's CMOS sensors are widely used in smartphone cameras, automotive applications, security cameras, surveillance systems, and smart home devices. They are also employed in robotics, industrial imaging, and IoT applications. The sensors' versatility allows them to adapt to various environments and functional requirements, from mobile device cameras to advanced automotive sensing systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Identify your specific application requirements including resolution, operating environment, and integration constraints to select the most suitable CMOS sensor from our range.

products.name: 8MP 1/4" CMOS Sensor for Mobile
products.shortDescription: 8MP 1/4" CMOS sensor designed for mobile applications with high image quality and low power consumption.
descriptionParagraphs: The GC08A3 is an 8-megapixel CMOS image sensor specifically designed for mobile applications. It features a 1/4" sensor size with 1.12μm pixel size, enabling high-quality imaging in compact form factors. The sensor supports MIPI CSI-2 interface and delivers exceptional performance in various lighting conditions.

This sensor incorporates advanced image processing algorithms that enhance color accuracy and dynamic range. Its low-power design is optimized for battery-powered mobile devices, with typical power consumption below 50mA. The GC08A3 offers excellent low-light performance and reduced noise in challenging environments.

Suitable for smartphone cameras, tablet cameras, and other mobile imaging applications, the GC08A3 provides reliable performance with compact packaging and low power consumption. Its high sensitivity ensures clear images even in dim lighting conditions.

faeReview.author: Wang Wei
faeReview.content: In our experience with smartphone camera modules, the GC08A3 offers an excellent balance of performance and cost-effectiveness. Its high sensitivity and good color reproduction make it a preferred choice for mid-range smartphone cameras. The sensor's low power consumption is particularly beneficial for battery life optimization. During our sample evaluation, it demonstrated stable performance across various lighting conditions, making it suitable for real-world mobile applications. The interface is straightforward to implement, reducing development time for camera modules.
highlight: Excellent value in mid-range smartphone camera applications
Low power consumption extends battery life

alternativeParts.reason: Identical to GC08A3 but with updated firmware
alternativeParts.comparison: Same electrical specs with firmware improvements

alternativeParts.reason: Higher resolution sensor with comparable performance
alternativeParts.comparison: 13MP vs 8MP resolution with same interface and package

faqs.question: What is the power consumption of GC08A3?
faqs.answer: The GC08A3 has typical power consumption of 45mA at 1.8V supply voltage, making it suitable for battery-powered mobile devices. Power consumption may vary based on frame rate and operating mode.
faqs.decisionGuide: For battery-powered applications, the GC08A3's low power consumption helps extend device runtime. Consider implementing power management features for optimal battery life.

faqs.question: What is the optical format of GC08A3 and why does it matter?
faqs.answer: The GC08A3 features a 1/4 inch optical format, which is a standard size for mobile camera modules. This compact format allows for slim smartphone designs while maintaining good light gathering capability. The 1/4 inch format is widely supported by lens manufacturers, making it easy to find compatible lenses for your camera module design.
faqs.decisionGuide: When designing a camera module, ensure your lens is compatible with the 1/4 inch optical format. This format offers a good balance between image quality and module thickness for mobile applications.

faqs.question: Does GC08A3 support autofocus functionality?
faqs.answer: Yes, the GC08A3 supports both contrast-based and PDAF (Phase Detection Auto Focus) autofocus modes. The sensor is compatible with VCM (Voice Coil Motor) actuators commonly used in smartphone cameras. This enables fast and accurate focusing for mobile photography applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: For autofocus applications, pair the GC08A3 with a compatible VCM actuator and ensure your ISP supports the chosen autofocus algorithm.

products.name: 13MP 1/3" CMOS Sensor for Smartphone
products.shortDescription: 13MP 1/3" CMOS sensor for smartphone applications with enhanced image quality and advanced features.
descriptionParagraphs: The GC13A0 is a high-resolution 13-megapixel CMOS image sensor designed specifically for smartphone applications. With a 1/3" sensor size, it provides excellent image quality and performance. The sensor features a 1.12μm pixel size optimized for improved low-light performance and reduced noise in challenging conditions.

This sensor integrates advanced image processing capabilities with HDR support, enabling enhanced color accuracy and dynamic range. Its MIPI CSI-2 interface allows seamless integration into smartphone platforms, while its compact packaging supports slim device designs. The GC13A0 is engineered for both mobile and industrial imaging applications.

The GC13A0 offers superior performance in both daylight and low-light conditions, thanks to its optimized pixel design and advanced signal processing. Its ultra-high resolution and wide dynamic range make it an ideal choice for premium smartphone cameras requiring detailed image capture in various lighting conditions.

faeReview.author: Chen Ming
faeReview.content: The GC13A0 is a standout in our testing for smartphone applications, delivering impressive image quality with its 13MP resolution and 1/3" sensor format. The HDR capabilities are particularly valuable in mobile photography scenarios, especially when capturing both bright skies and darker objects in the same frame. Its power efficiency is commendable for maintaining battery life during extended use. For customers requiring high-quality smartphone cameras, this sensor provides exceptional value with its comprehensive feature set. The integration is straightforward with standard MIPI interfaces, making it compatible with most mobile platforms.
highlight: High-resolution smartphone sensor with advanced HDR capabilities
Excellent image quality in various lighting conditions

alternativeParts.reason: Lower resolution sensor for cost-sensitive applications
alternativeParts.comparison: 8MP vs 13MP with same interface and lower cost

alternativeParts.reason: Updated version with enhanced low-light performance
alternativeParts.comparison: Improved pixel technology for better low-light performance

companionParts.relationship: 4MP IoT camera sensor with AI features

faqs.question: Does GC13A0 support HDR imaging?
faqs.answer: Yes, the GC13A0 supports HDR (High Dynamic Range) imaging, enabling better detail capture in both bright and dark areas of the scene. This feature is particularly valuable for smartphone photography in challenging lighting conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Enable HDR mode for scenes with high contrast between bright and dark areas. The HDR feature helps capture more detail in shadows and highlights.

faqs.question: What makes GC13A0 suitable for premium smartphones?
faqs.answer: The GC13A0's 13MP resolution combined with 1.12μm pixel size delivers excellent image quality for premium smartphone cameras. Its advanced image processing capabilities, HDR support, and MIPI CSI-2 interface make it ideal for high-end mobile applications. The sensor's low power consumption and compact packaging also support slim smartphone designs while maintaining battery efficiency.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose GC13A0 for premium smartphone applications requiring high resolution and advanced features. The sensor balances performance and power efficiency for flagship devices.

faqs.question: What is the frame rate capability of GC13A0?
faqs.answer: The GC13A0 supports up to 30fps at full 13MP resolution. For applications requiring higher frame rates, the sensor can output lower resolutions at increased frame rates. This flexibility makes it suitable for both photography and video recording applications in smartphones.
faqs.decisionGuide: For video applications, consider the required resolution and frame rate combination. The GC13A0 offers flexible output configurations to meet various application needs.

categories.name: Display Driver ICs
categories.description: High-performance display driver ICs for LCD and AMOLED panels, supporting HD+ to FHD+ resolutions. Galaxycore's display drivers deliver excellent image quality with low power consumption for smartphones, tablets, and wearable devices.
categories.longDescription: Galaxycore's Display Driver ICs provide comprehensive solutions for driving LCD and AMOLED displays in mobile and consumer electronics. Our product range includes HD+ LCD drivers and FHD+ AMOLED drivers designed for optimal performance and power efficiency. These drivers support various panel technologies and offer advanced features like dynamic refresh rate control, enhanced color accuracy, and reduced power consumption. As an authorized distributor, LiTong Electronics provides technical support for display driver selection and integration.
categories.selectionGuide: Guide to selecting the optimal display driver IC for your LCD or AMOLED panel requirements

faqs.question: What display resolutions are supported by Galaxycore drivers?
faqs.answer: Galaxycore display drivers support resolutions from HD+ (720x1600) to FHD+ (1080x2400) for mobile applications. The GC9503 series supports HD+ LCD panels, while the GC9702 series drives FHD+ AMOLED displays. These drivers are optimized for smartphone and tablet applications with excellent color reproduction and power efficiency.
faqs.decisionGuide: Match the driver IC to your panel resolution. Use GC9503 for HD+ LCD panels and GC9702 for FHD+ AMOLED displays.

faqs.question: What interfaces do Galaxycore display drivers support?
faqs.answer: Galaxycore display drivers support industry-standard interfaces including MIPI DSI for high-speed data transmission, I2C for configuration, and SPI for control signals. The MIPI DSI interface supports multiple lanes (1-4 lanes) for bandwidth optimization based on resolution and refresh rate requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ensure your application processor supports MIPI DSI interface. Most modern mobile processors are compatible with Galaxycore drivers.

faqs.question: Are Galaxycore display drivers suitable for automotive applications?
faqs.answer: While primarily designed for consumer electronics, some Galaxycore display drivers can be adapted for automotive infotainment systems. For strict automotive applications requiring AEC-Q100 qualification, please contact our FAE team to discuss specific requirements and available options.
faqs.decisionGuide: For automotive display applications, verify temperature range and reliability requirements. Contact us for automotive-qualified options.

faqs.question: What is the typical power consumption of these display drivers?
faqs.answer: Galaxycore display drivers feature industry-leading power efficiency. The GC9503 HD+ driver consumes approximately 15mW in typical operation, while the GC9702 FHD+ AMOLED driver consumes around 25mW. Both drivers include power-saving modes and dynamic refresh rate control to minimize power consumption during static content display.
faqs.decisionGuide: For battery-powered devices, these low-power drivers help extend runtime. Consider implementing dynamic refresh rate control for additional power savings.

faqs.question: What package types are available for display drivers?
faqs.answer: Galaxycore display drivers are available in compact CSP and COF packages optimized for space-constrained mobile devices. The GC9503 uses a 6.5x4.5mm CSP package, while the GC9702 uses a 7.2x5.0mm COF package. These packages support high-density interconnects required for high-resolution displays.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select package based on your PCB layout constraints and manufacturing capabilities. COF packages are ideal for ultra-thin device designs.

faqs.question: Do Galaxycore drivers support touch integration?
faqs.answer: Yes, Galaxycore display drivers support integrated touch functionality through TDDI (Touch and Display Driver Integration) technology. This integration reduces component count, simplifies PCB design, and improves touch response time by eliminating communication delays between separate touch and display controllers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider TDDI solutions for new designs to reduce BOM cost and improve touch performance. Contact us for TDDI-compatible driver options.

products.name: HD+ LCD Display Driver
products.shortDescription: High-performance HD+ LCD display driver with MIPI DSI interface and low power consumption.
descriptionParagraphs: The GC9503 is a high-performance display driver IC designed for HD+ (720x1600) LCD panels. It features a 4-lane MIPI DSI interface for high-speed data transmission and supports up to 60Hz refresh rate. The driver incorporates advanced image processing algorithms for enhanced color accuracy and smooth motion display.

This driver IC includes multiple power-saving modes and dynamic refresh rate control, making it ideal for battery-powered mobile devices. The GC9503 supports 16.7 million colors with 8-bit color depth and offers excellent contrast ratio for vibrant image display.

With its compact CSP package and low EMI design, the GC9503 is perfect for slim smartphone designs. The driver supports various panel technologies including a-Si and LTPS LCDs, providing flexibility for different product positioning strategies.

faeReview.author: Li Hua
faeReview.content: The GC9503 has proven to be a reliable choice for mid-range smartphone displays. Its MIPI DSI interface integrates seamlessly with mainstream mobile processors, and the power consumption figures match specifications closely in real-world testing. The driver's image quality tuning options provide good flexibility for different panel characteristics. We've successfully integrated this driver in multiple smartphone projects with excellent results in both performance and reliability.

alternativeParts.comparison: Same resolution support with slightly higher power consumption
FHD+ AMOLED vs HD+ LCD support

faqs.question: What panel types are compatible with GC9503?
faqs.answer: The GC9503 is compatible with a-Si (amorphous silicon) and LTPS (Low Temperature Poly-Silicon) LCD panels. It supports various panel configurations and can be tuned for different panel characteristics through register configuration.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify your panel type and timing requirements. The GC9503 supports most standard HD+ LCD panels with proper initialization.

faqs.question: What is the typical power consumption of GC9503?
faqs.answer: The GC9503 consumes approximately 15mW in typical operation, making it ideal for battery-powered mobile devices. It includes multiple power-saving modes and dynamic refresh rate control to minimize power consumption during static content display.
faqs.decisionGuide: For battery-powered devices, the low power consumption helps extend runtime. Consider implementing dynamic refresh rate control for additional power savings.

faqs.question: Does GC9503 support ESD protection?
faqs.answer: Yes, the GC9503 includes integrated ESD protection on all input/output pins, meeting industry standards for mobile applications. This protects the driver IC from electrostatic discharge during manufacturing and normal operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: The built-in ESD protection simplifies system design and reduces the need for external protection components.

products.name: FHD+ AMOLED Display Driver
products.shortDescription: Advanced FHD+ AMOLED display driver with HDR support and ultra-low power consumption.
descriptionParagraphs: The GC9702 is a premium display driver IC designed for FHD+ (1080x2400) AMOLED panels. It features advanced HDR (High Dynamic Range) support with enhanced brightness control and color accuracy. The 4-lane MIPI DSI interface enables high-speed data transmission for smooth 60Hz and 90Hz refresh rates.

This driver incorporates sophisticated power management with per-pixel brightness control, enabling significant power savings for AMOLED displays. The GC9702 supports 1.07 billion colors with 10-bit color depth and features advanced image processing for motion smoothing and color enhancement.

With support for always-on display functionality and ultra-low power standby modes, the GC9702 is ideal for premium smartphones requiring high-quality displays with extended battery life. The driver's COF package enables ultra-thin device designs.

faeReview.author: Zhang Wei
faeReview.content: The GC9702 delivers exceptional performance for premium AMOLED displays. The HDR support significantly enhances video viewing experience, and the power management features help extend battery life in real-world usage. During our evaluation, the driver's always-on display functionality worked flawlessly with minimal power impact. The color accuracy and motion handling are comparable to drivers from leading competitors. Integration with Snapdragon and MediaTek platforms was straightforward with standard MIPI DSI configuration.
highlight: Excellent HDR performance for video content
Premium color accuracy and motion handling

alternativeParts.reason: Lower resolution LCD driver for cost savings
alternativeParts.comparison: HD+ LCD vs FHD+ AMOLED support

faqs.question: Does GC9702 support high refresh rate displays?
faqs.answer: Yes, the GC9702 supports both 60Hz and 90Hz refresh rates for smooth scrolling and gaming experiences. The driver automatically adjusts refresh rate based on content to optimize power consumption.
faqs.decisionGuide: Enable 90Hz mode for gaming and smooth UI experiences. Use adaptive refresh rate for optimal battery life.

faqs.question: What HDR formats does GC9702 support?
faqs.answer: The GC9702 supports HDR10 format for enhanced video viewing experience. It features advanced brightness control and color accuracy for HDR content, providing better contrast and more vivid colors compared to standard dynamic range displays.
faqs.decisionGuide: Enable HDR mode when playing HDR content for the best viewing experience. The driver's HDR support significantly enhances video quality.

faqs.question: Is GC9702 suitable for always-on display applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, the GC9702 is specifically designed for always-on display functionality with ultra-low power standby modes. The per-pixel brightness control enables significant power savings when displaying static content, making it ideal for smartphone always-on displays.
faqs.decisionGuide: For always-on display applications, the GC9702's low power standby mode helps extend battery life while providing convenient time and notification display.

categories.name: Automotive Image Sensors
categories.description: AEC-Q100 qualified CMOS image sensors designed for automotive applications including ADAS, surround view systems, and in-cabin monitoring. These sensors meet stringent automotive reliability standards.
categories.longDescription: Galaxycore's Automotive Image Sensors are specifically designed and qualified for automotive applications requiring high reliability and performance. As an authorized Galaxycore distributor, LiTong Electronics provides comprehensive product selection guidance and technical support for automotive imaging applications. All automotive sensors meet AEC-Q100 Grade 2 or Grade 3 standards, ensuring operation across automotive temperature ranges (-40°C to +105°C). These sensors are ideal for advanced driver assistance systems (ADAS), surround view cameras, rear-view cameras, and driver monitoring systems. With features like HDR, LED flicker mitigation, and high sensitivity, they provide reliable imaging in challenging automotive lighting conditions.
categories.selectionGuide: Automotive image sensor selection guide for ADAS and surround view applications

faqs.question: What AEC-Q100 grades are available for Galaxycore automotive sensors?
faqs.answer: Galaxycore automotive image sensors are available in AEC-Q100 Grade 2 (-40°C to +105°C) and Grade 3 (-40°C to +85°C) qualifications. Grade 2 sensors are recommended for engine compartment and exterior applications, while Grade 3 sensors are suitable for interior cabin applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose Grade 2 for exterior and harsh environment applications. Grade 3 is sufficient for interior cabin monitoring applications.

faqs.question: Do these sensors support HDR for challenging lighting conditions?
faqs.answer: Yes, all Galaxycore automotive sensors support HDR (High Dynamic Range) imaging with multiple exposure modes. This enables clear imaging in high-contrast scenarios such as entering/exiting tunnels, driving toward sunset, and nighttime driving with oncoming headlights.
faqs.decisionGuide: Enable HDR mode for all automotive applications to ensure reliable imaging across varying lighting conditions.

faqs.question: What is LED flicker mitigation and why is it important?
faqs.answer: LED flicker mitigation (LFM) is a technology that prevents image flickering when capturing LED traffic lights, taillights, and signs. Without LFM, LED light sources may appear to flicker or be invisible in camera images due to their pulsing nature. Galaxycore automotive sensors include LFM technology to ensure reliable detection of LED signals.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ensure LED flicker mitigation is enabled for forward-facing cameras that need to detect traffic lights and vehicle lights.

faqs.question: Are these sensors suitable for surround view camera systems?
faqs.answer: Yes, Galaxycore automotive sensors are ideal for surround view systems. The 2MP GC2053-A provides sufficient resolution for parking assistance, while the 4MP GC4653-A offers enhanced detail for advanced surround view applications. Both sensors feature wide dynamic range and excellent low-light performance for reliable operation in parking garages and nighttime conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use GC2053-A for basic surround view systems. Choose GC4653-A for premium systems requiring higher resolution and detail.

faqs.question: What interface options are available for automotive sensors?
faqs.answer: Galaxycore automotive sensors support MIPI CSI-2 interface with 1-2 lanes for high-speed data transmission. They also support parallel DVP interface for compatibility with legacy systems. The MIPI interface includes error detection and correction features for reliable data transmission in automotive environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use MIPI CSI-2 for new designs to benefit from higher bandwidth and reliability features. DVP is available for legacy system compatibility.

faqs.question: Do these sensors meet functional safety requirements?
faqs.answer: Galaxycore automotive sensors are designed with functional safety considerations and support ASIL-B level applications when combined with appropriate system-level safety measures. The sensors include built-in self-test features and diagnostic capabilities required for safety-critical applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: For ASIL-B and higher safety requirements, implement appropriate system-level safety measures and contact our automotive FAE team for guidance.

products.name: 2MP AEC-Q100 Automotive Image Sensor
products.shortDescription: AEC-Q100 Grade 2 qualified 2MP automotive image sensor with HDR and LED flicker mitigation.
descriptionParagraphs: The GC2053-A is a 2-megapixel automotive-grade CMOS image sensor qualified to AEC-Q100 Grade 2 standards. It operates reliably across the full automotive temperature range of -40°C to +105°C, making it suitable for exterior camera applications including rear-view cameras, surround view systems, and forward-facing ADAS cameras.

This sensor features advanced HDR technology with up to 120dB dynamic range, enabling clear imaging in high-contrast scenarios. The built-in LED flicker mitigation ensures reliable detection of LED traffic lights and vehicle lights. With 1/2.8" optical format and 2.8μm pixels, it delivers excellent low-light performance for nighttime driving applications.

The GC2053-A supports MIPI CSI-2 interface with error detection and correction, ensuring reliable data transmission in automotive electrical environments. Its compact package and robust design make it ideal for space-constrained automotive camera modules.

faeReview.author: Liu Jun
faeReview.content: The GC2053-A has been extensively tested in automotive environments and consistently meets AEC-Q100 Grade 2 requirements. The HDR performance is impressive, capturing clear images when transitioning between bright sunlight and dark tunnels. LED flicker mitigation works effectively with modern LED traffic lights and vehicle lighting. We've successfully integrated this sensor in multiple Tier 1 automotive projects for rear-view and surround view cameras. The reliability and image quality meet OEM requirements for mass production.

faqs.question: What is the minimum illumination for GC2053-A?
faqs.answer: The GC2053-A achieves excellent low-light performance with minimum illumination of 0.1 lux in color mode and 0.01 lux in monochrome mode. This enables reliable nighttime driving assistance.
faqs.decisionGuide: For extremely low-light applications, consider using IR illumination to supplement the sensor's natural low-light capabilities.

faqs.question: What temperature range does GC2053-A support?
faqs.answer: The GC2053-A is qualified to AEC-Q100 Grade 2 standards, supporting operation from -40°C to +105°C. This wide temperature range makes it suitable for exterior automotive applications including rear-view cameras and surround view systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: For exterior automotive applications, ensure the sensor meets the required temperature grade. Grade 2 is suitable for most exterior mounting locations.

faqs.question: Does GC2053-A support functional safety requirements?
faqs.answer: Yes, the GC2053-A supports ASIL-B functional safety requirements when properly integrated with appropriate system-level safety measures. The sensor includes built-in self-test features and diagnostic capabilities required for safety-critical automotive applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: For ASIL-B applications, implement proper system-level safety measures and contact our automotive FAE team for integration guidance.

products.name: 4MP AEC-Q100 Automotive Image Sensor
products.shortDescription: High-resolution 4MP automotive image sensor with AEC-Q100 Grade 2 qualification for advanced ADAS applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The GC4653-A is a high-resolution 4-megapixel automotive-grade CMOS image sensor qualified to AEC-Q100 Grade 2 standards. With its 1/2.7" optical format and advanced pixel technology, it delivers superior image quality for demanding automotive applications including advanced ADAS, high-resolution surround view systems, and autonomous driving cameras.

This sensor features enhanced HDR capability with up to 120dB dynamic range and advanced LED flicker mitigation technology. The 2.0μm pixels provide excellent sensitivity and low noise performance across the automotive temperature range. The GC4653-A supports multiple output formats and frame rates for flexible system integration.

Designed for safety-critical applications, the GC4653-A includes comprehensive built-in self-test features and supports ASIL-B functional safety requirements when properly integrated. Its robust design ensures reliable operation in harsh automotive environments including vibration, shock, and extreme temperatures.

faeReview.author: Chen Wei
faeReview.content: The GC4653-A represents a significant step forward in automotive imaging. The 4MP resolution provides the detail needed for advanced ADAS features like pedestrian detection and traffic sign recognition. The sensor's reliability in extreme temperature testing has been excellent, with consistent performance from -40°C to +105°C. The HDR performance handles challenging lighting scenarios effectively, and the LED flicker mitigation is crucial for modern traffic environments. This sensor has been successfully qualified by multiple automotive OEMs for production programs.
highlight: High resolution for advanced ADAS features

faqs.question: Is GC4653-A suitable for autonomous driving applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, the GC4653-A is designed for autonomous driving camera systems. Its 4MP resolution, AEC-Q100 Grade 2 qualification, and ASIL-B support make it suitable for safety-critical autonomous driving perception systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: For autonomous driving, implement multiple sensors with redundancy. The GC4653-A provides the resolution and reliability needed for perception systems.

faqs.question: What is the dynamic range of GC4653-A?
faqs.answer: The GC4653-A features up to 120dB HDR dynamic range, enabling clear imaging in high-contrast scenarios such as entering tunnels, driving toward sunset, and nighttime driving with oncoming headlights. This wide dynamic range is essential for reliable ADAS performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Enable HDR mode for all automotive applications to ensure reliable imaging across varying lighting conditions. The 120dB dynamic range handles challenging scenarios effectively.

faqs.question: Does GC4653-A support LED flicker mitigation?
faqs.answer: Yes, the GC4653-A includes advanced LED flicker mitigation (LFM) technology. This ensures reliable detection of LED traffic lights, taillights, and signs that may appear to flicker or be invisible without LFM due to their pulsing nature.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ensure LED flicker mitigation is enabled for forward-facing cameras that need to detect traffic lights and vehicle lights. This is critical for ADAS and autonomous driving applications.

categories.name: Security & IoT Sensors
categories.description: Specialized CMOS image sensors optimized for security cameras, smart home devices, and IoT applications. Features include excellent low-light performance, AI-ready interfaces, and power efficiency.
categories.longDescription: Galaxycore's Security & IoT Sensors are specifically designed for surveillance, smart home, and Internet of Things applications. As an authorized Galaxycore distributor, LiTong Electronics provides comprehensive selection guidance and technical support for these specialized sensors. These sensors combine excellent image quality with power efficiency and intelligent features required for modern connected devices. With resolutions from 2MP to 4MP, they support AI-powered analytics, facial recognition, and object detection applications. The sensors feature advanced low-light performance, WDR (Wide Dynamic Range), and smart IR modes for reliable 24/7 operation in security applications.
categories.selectionGuide: Security and IoT camera sensor selection guide for surveillance and smart home applications

faqs.question: What makes these sensors suitable for 24/7 security applications?
faqs.answer: Galaxycore security sensors feature excellent low-light sensitivity (down to 0.001 lux), smart IR modes for night vision, and WDR for handling backlight conditions. They also include power-efficient designs for battery-powered cameras and support AI acceleration interfaces for intelligent analytics.
faqs.decisionGuide: For 24/7 operation, select sensors with good low-light performance and consider IR illumination for complete darkness scenarios.

faqs.question: Do these sensors support AI and machine learning applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, Galaxycore security sensors support AI-ready interfaces that work with edge AI processors for real-time analytics. They provide high-quality image data optimized for facial recognition, object detection, and behavior analysis algorithms.
faqs.decisionGuide: For AI applications, pair these sensors with compatible AI processors. The high image quality improves AI detection accuracy.

faqs.question: What is WDR and when is it needed?
faqs.answer: WDR (Wide Dynamic Range) is a technology that captures clear images in high-contrast lighting conditions, such as when a camera faces a bright window or entrance. Galaxycore security sensors support up to 100dB WDR for challenging lighting environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Enable WDR for cameras monitoring entrances, windows, or areas with mixed lighting conditions.

faqs.question: Are these sensors suitable for battery-powered cameras?
faqs.answer: Yes, Galaxycore IoT sensors feature ultra-low power consumption modes including standby modes below 1mW and efficient active operation. They support motion-activated wake-up for battery-powered security cameras requiring extended operation between charges.
faqs.decisionGuide: For battery-powered applications, use motion detection to keep the sensor in low-power standby mode until activation is needed.

faqs.question: What resolutions are available for security applications?
faqs.answer: Galaxycore offers 2MP (GC2063) and 4MP (GC4663) sensors for security applications. The 2MP resolution is suitable for general surveillance, while 4MP provides enhanced detail for facial recognition and license plate reading applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose 2MP for general surveillance and cost-sensitive applications. Select 4MP for applications requiring fine detail recognition.

faqs.question: Do these sensors support smart home integration?
faqs.answer: Yes, Galaxycore IoT sensors are designed for smart home integration with compact packages, low power consumption, and interfaces compatible with popular smart home platforms. They work with WiFi, Zigbee, and Z-Wave connected camera systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: These sensors integrate well with major smart home platforms. Verify interface compatibility with your chosen connectivity solution.

products.name: 2MP Security & IoT Image Sensor
products.shortDescription: High-sensitivity 2MP image sensor optimized for security cameras and IoT devices with excellent low-light performance.
descriptionParagraphs: The GC2063 is a 2-megapixel CMOS image sensor specifically designed for security camera and IoT applications. It features a 1/2.9" optical format with 2.8μm pixels optimized for exceptional low-light sensitivity. The sensor achieves 0.001 lux minimum illumination, enabling clear night vision even in near-total darkness.

This sensor includes advanced WDR technology with 100dB dynamic range for handling challenging backlight conditions. The smart IR mode automatically adjusts settings for IR-illuminated night vision. With ultra-low power consumption of 80mW in active mode and sub-1mW standby, it's ideal for battery-powered wireless cameras.

The GC2063 supports DVP and MIPI interfaces for flexible integration with various SoC platforms. Its compact package and wide operating temperature range (-20°C to +70°C) make it suitable for both indoor and outdoor security applications.

faeReview.author: Wu Jian
faeReview.content: The GC2063 has become our go-to sensor for security camera applications. The low-light performance is exceptional - we've achieved usable images in conditions where other sensors fail completely. The WDR handles entrance monitoring scenarios very well, capturing both indoor and outdoor details simultaneously. Power consumption is as specified, making it suitable for battery-powered doorbell cameras we've designed. The interface flexibility allows integration with various SoC platforms from different manufacturers.

alternativeParts.reason: Similar sensor with slightly different specs
alternativeParts.comparison: Similar low-light performance with different package

companionParts.relationship: Mobile sensor for smart home integration
Display driver for camera monitor systems

faqs.question: What is the maximum cable length for GC2063?
faqs.answer: With MIPI CSI-2 interface, the GC2063 supports cable lengths up to 30cm with standard flex cables. For longer distances, use MIPI signal conditioning or consider DVP interface which supports longer cables at lower data rates.
faqs.decisionGuide: For long cable runs, consider using an ISP chip with DVP output or implement signal conditioning for MIPI interfaces.

faqs.question: What is the minimum illumination for GC2063?
faqs.answer: The GC2063 achieves exceptional low-light performance with minimum illumination of 0.001 lux, enabling clear night vision even in near-total darkness. This makes it ideal for 24/7 security applications without additional IR illumination in many scenarios.
faqs.decisionGuide: For extremely low-light security applications, the GC2063's high sensitivity reduces the need for supplemental lighting. Consider IR illumination only for complete darkness scenarios.

faqs.question: Does GC2063 support WDR for backlight scenarios?
faqs.answer: Yes, the GC2063 features 100dB WDR (Wide Dynamic Range) technology, enabling clear imaging in high-contrast lighting conditions such as entrances with bright outdoor light and darker indoor areas. This is essential for security cameras monitoring doorways and windows.
faqs.decisionGuide: Enable WDR mode for cameras monitoring entrances, windows, or areas with mixed lighting conditions. The 100dB WDR captures detail in both bright and dark areas simultaneously.

products.name: 4MP AI-Ready Security Image Sensor
products.shortDescription: Advanced 4MP image sensor with AI acceleration and analytics for intelligent security systems.
descriptionParagraphs: The GC4663 is a high-performance 4-megapixel CMOS image sensor designed for AI-powered security and IoT applications. With its 1/2.7" optical format and advanced 2.0μm pixels, it delivers exceptional image quality for facial recognition, license plate reading, and object detection applications.

This sensor features AI-ready output formats optimized for edge AI processors, reducing bandwidth requirements while maintaining detection accuracy. The enhanced WDR (100dB) and advanced noise reduction enable reliable operation in challenging lighting conditions. Smart ROI (Region of Interest) modes allow focused high-resolution capture of detected objects.

The GC4663 includes integrated motion detection with programmable zones for event-triggered recording. Its low-latency output and high frame rate (up to 60fps at 1080p) support real-time analytics applications. The sensor's power-efficient design makes it suitable for always-on AI camera systems.

faeReview.author: Zhao Lei
faeReview.content: The GC4663 is an excellent choice for AI-powered security cameras. The AI-ready output formats significantly reduce processing load on edge AI chips while maintaining detection accuracy. We've tested it with several AI accelerators and achieved real-time facial recognition with high accuracy. The 4MP resolution provides the detail needed for license plate reading at reasonable distances. Motion detection integration works well for event-triggered recording, reducing storage and bandwidth requirements. Overall, a strong sensor for intelligent security applications.

companionParts.relationship: High-res mobile sensor for hybrid systems
AMOLED driver for smart doorbell displays

faqs.question: Which AI processors are compatible with GC4663?
faqs.answer: The GC4663 is compatible with major edge AI processors including Ambarella, HiSilicon, Rockchip, and MediaTek AI platforms. The MIPI CSI-2 interface and AI-ready output formats work with most modern AI SoCs designed for camera applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify compatibility with your chosen AI processor. The sensor works best with processors supporting MIPI CSI-2 and hardware AI acceleration.

faqs.question: What AI applications is GC4663 optimized for?
faqs.answer: The GC4663 is optimized for AI-powered security applications including facial recognition, license plate reading, object detection, and behavior analysis. Its AI-ready output formats reduce bandwidth and processing requirements while maintaining high detection accuracy for edge AI applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: For AI security applications, pair the GC4663 with a compatible edge AI processor. The sensor's optimized output formats improve detection accuracy and reduce system latency.

faqs.question: Does GC4663 support motion detection for event-triggered recording?
faqs.answer: Yes, the GC4663 includes integrated motion detection with programmable zones for event-triggered recording. This feature reduces storage and bandwidth requirements by only recording when motion is detected in specified areas, making it ideal for 24/7 security monitoring.
faqs.decisionGuide: Configure motion detection zones based on your monitoring requirements. This feature significantly reduces storage costs while ensuring important events are captured.
